name: Mee Krob (Thai Crisp Fried Noodles)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil for deep frying
- 1 large onion, finely chopped
- 5 garlic cloves, finely minced
- 1/2 pound pork fillet, sliced into 1-inch long pieces
- 1 whole chicken breast, boned and sliced into 1-inch long pieces
- 6 dried Chinese mushrooms (shiitake), soaked and sliced
- 2 small fresh chilies, seeded and sliced
- 3 tablespoons soy sauce
- Juice of 2 limes
- 2 tablespoons rice vinegar
- 3 tablespoons sugar (granulated or brown)
- 3 tablespoons fish sauce
- 3 tablespoons cooked shrimp (small), either raw or cooked
- 4 eggs, beaten
- Handful of bean sprouts
- 6 green onions, finely chopped
- 2 green onions, cut into rings
2. Tear the noodles into handfuls and place them in a large shopping bag to avoid a mess.
3. Heat oil and fry the noodles until they puff up and turn milky white. Flip over for a few seconds, drain in the bag, and repeat with the remaining pieces.
4. Pour off all but 6 tablespoons of oil in a wok or deep frying pan.
5. Fry onions and garlic lightly.
6. Add pork and cook through. Then add chicken and fry until it turns white.
7. Put in mushrooms and chilies and reduce heat.
8. In a bowl, combine soy sauce, lime juice, rice vinegar, sugar, and fish sauce. Pour into the wok and simmer until the liquid is reduced by half and starts to turn syrupy.
9. Add shrimp and cook for 1 minute.
10. Push the contents of the wok to the sides and pour beaten eggs into the center. Let set for a minute, then stir and add bean sprouts and drained noodles. Toss lightly (the noodles are fragile) to mix ingredients.
11. Garnish with cilantro and green onions.
Note: Rice vermicelli are also known as rice sticks or cellophane noodles depending on where you find them. Use the thinnest variety available, as other kinds of noodles will not work. The oil must be fresh and hot (375F to 400F) for the best results. This recipe serves 6-8 people; halve it for fewer servings.
Posted by Stephen Ceideberg; September 28 1992.
